name: Turkish Baklava
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp Ground Cinnamon or 1/4 tsp Ground and 1 Stick Cinnamon
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop walnuts into medium fine pieces and mix with 1 cup sugar, 1/4 teaspoon ground cinnamon, and 1/4 teaspoon ground cloves.
2. Melt butter.
3. Cut fillo in half so each sheet fits the bottom of the baking pan.
4. Generously butter the bottom and sides of the baking pan and place the fillo one sheet at a time, in the baking pan. Generously spread butter on each sheet of fillo as it is placed in the pan. Every 6 to 8 sheets of fillo, spread a layer of walnut mixture.
5. Continue layering filo sheets and walnuts until all fillo is used. Pour any remaining butter over top of the baklava.
6. The secret to good flaky baklava is to have each sheet of fillo well buttered so that the sheets do not stick together.
7. With a sharp knife, cut the baklava diagonally to form diamond shaped pieces.
8. Preheat oven to 350 degrees and bake the baklava for 40 to 45 minutes or until golden brown on top. Remove from oven and allow to cool completely.
9. To make syrup, mix one cup water, two cups sugar, 1/4 teaspoon ground cloves, and either 3/4 teaspoon cinnamon or a cinnamon stick in a saucepan. Bring to a boil and simmer for 20 minutes.
10. Pour the boiling syrup over the cool baklava and allow to cool completely again. When cool, serve individual pieces with strong (Turkish) sweet coffee and dried apricots for an exquisite dessert.
